name: Sucrose Gradient Solutions (10–70% for Organelle Isolation)
nameCn: 蔗糖密度梯度液（10–70%，细胞器分离用）

components.name: Sucrose
components.note: Prepare each density separately: 10%=10 g, 20%=20 g, 30%=30 g, 40%=40 g, 50%=50 g, 60%=60 g, 70%=70 g per 100 mL

components.name: HEPES-KOH (1 M, pH 7.4)
components.note: 10 mM final; or use 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4

components.name: EDTA (0.5 M, pH 8.0)
components.note: 1 mM final; Ca²⁺ chelation protects organelle membranes

notes.en:
Sucrose density (approx, 20°C): 10%=1.040 g/mL, 20%=1.083, 30%=1.130, 40%=1.179, 50%=1.230, 60%=1.286, 70%=1.347.
Common layer configurations:
- ER microsomes: 10/20/30/40% (step) or 15–45% linear
- Golgi apparatus: 10/25/35% or linear 10–50%
- Lysosomes: 14.3%/19%/26% step; pellet after 100,000×g 1 h
- Exosomes: 30%/45%/60% step (or 10–70% continuous)
Prepare continuous gradient with gradient maker or freeze-thaw method (layer, freeze −80°C, thaw at 4°C = diffusion gradient). Store at 4°C up to 2 weeks. Filter 0.22 µm for RNase-free applications.

ref: Graham (2002) Curr Protoc Cell Biol 3.6.1–3.6.13; de Duve et al. (1955) Biochem J 60:604–617; Bhatt & Bhatt (2021) STAR Protoc 2:100450

prepSteps.step: Dissolve sucrose in HEPES or Tris buffer to desired concentration.
Add protease inhibitors if for protein work.
Store at 4°C; prepare fresh for best results.
